unmatched.name: playwright-self-healing-framework
unmatched.description: Self-healing Playwright E2E framework: intercepts broken locators, scores the live DOM against a semantic fingerprint, and rewrites selectors on the fly. TypeScript + Playwright + Vitest. Zero LLMs, zero API keys.
unmatched.readme:
Playwright Self-Healing Framework

> **Your E2E tests stop breaking when the UI changes.**
>
> A TypeScript-first Playwright framework that intercepts failing locators, scores every node in the live DOM against a semantic fingerprint, and rewrites broken selectors on the fly — deterministically, offline, zero LLM calls.

 
 
 
 
 
 

---

See it heal — in 30 seconds

Same test. Three rewrites of the same page. **Zero maintenance.**

 

|                         | original.html          | mutated.html        | text-drift.html   |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------- | ------------------- |
| submit button id      | #submit-order          | #btn_a1b2c3d4       | #placeOrderBtn    |
| submit button class   | submit-btn             | cta-btn primary-cta | cta-btn           |
| visible text            | Submit Order           | Submit Order        | **Place Order**   |
| data-testid           | checkout-submit        | _(removed)_           | _(removed)_         |
| email label             | Email address          | Email address       | **Email**         |

Heal report produced by the actual test run in this repo:

 

Three DOMs, one test file, six successful heals — and a full JSON audit trail for every decision.

---

Why this exists

Every E2E suite eventually becomes a **selector maintenance job**. A dev renames an id, swaps a CSS framework, or migrates to a new component library — and twelve green tests flip red overnight. Teams respond with:

**Instrumentation tax** — stamping data-testid on every interactive element, forever.
**Ret...

CBrowser — Cognitive Browser Automation

**No API key needed** for basic commands (navigate, screenshot, click, extract, explore). Cognitive journeys require an Anthropic API key:

 

**Check your environment:** Run npx cbrowser doctor to verify everything is set up correctly.

**Accessibility:** CBrowser supports --no-color (or NO_COLOR env var), --plain (no emoji/decorations), and --json-output (structured JSON) for screen readers, CI pipelines, and scripting.

unmatched.name: aws-test-framework-boilerplate
unmatched.description: Reference architecture for a unified test framework spanning AWS backend integration, Playwright UI, and synthetic user-journey agents. TypeScript monorepo with self-healing selectors and CircleCI orchestration.
unmatched.readme:
AWS Test Framework — Architectural Reference

A unified architecture for AWS-backed API testing, Playwright UI testing, and synthetic user journey simulation, routed through a CircleCI pipeline that scales to N teams and environments.

> This repository is an **architectural reference**, not a runnable project. It documents how the pieces fit together, what the interfaces look like, and how the CI pipeline decomposes work. Deployment requires engagement.

---

What's demonstrated

| Layer | Pattern |
|---|---|
| **AWS-native API testing** | SigV4-signed requests, Secrets Manager, DynamoDB, Lambda, SQS, Athena, API Gateway, CloudWatch |
| **Multi-team / multi-env** | Configurable team list, three environments, dynamically routed CircleCI pipeline |
| **Self-healing UI tests** | Playwright Page Object Model backed by a selector registry with ordered fallback strategies |
| **Synthetic user journeys** | Black-box intelligence layer producing session reports with emotional arc and root-cause analysis |
| **Rich reporting** | Allure + Mochawesome + JUnit, published to S3 with Slack notifications |
| **Dynamic CI** | CircleCI setup continuation routes each commit to the right workflow |

---

How to read this repo in 10 minutes

An ordered tour for someone evaluating the architecture:

test/services/ — AWS QA integration patterns (5 min)

The technical core. Start with generic-api-fetch.service.ts and aws-credentials.service.ts — these two files show the canonical signed-request pattern and the credential-resolution algorithm that handles both local AWS SSO and CI static-IAM. The...

unmatched.name: online-portfolio
unmatched.description: static website for my online cv/portfolio. Features pytest playwright test automation framework with self healing selector logic.
unmatched.readme:
**Overview**

Static website for my online cv and portfolio.  Also includes a python playwright test automation framework. This framework includes an AI-assisted selector healing mechanism designed to improve test stability when UI changes break existing locators.

It follows a structured fallback approach:

Primary selector 
Alternative selectors
AI-generated selector based on DOM context

When a valid selector is found, it is reused. If AI successfully heals a selector, it is automatically persisted for future runs.

**How it works**

Selector Resolution

Each element is defined in a selector store:

{
  "automation_projects": {
    "primary": "#projects-personal .card",
    "alternatives": [
      "#projects_personal .card",
      ".projects-personal .card"
    ]
  }
}

The framework attempts each selector until a valid match is found.

Validation

Selectors are validated by checking that at least one element is present:

locator.first.wait_for(timeout=1000)

This avoids false positives where a locator exists but matches no elements.

AI Healing

If all selectors fail:

The current DOM is captured
The broken selector is sent to the AI model
A new selector is generated based on DOM structure

Selector Normalization

AI responses are cleaned before use:

selector = selector.replace("```", "").strip()

This ensures compatibility with Playwright.

Persistence

If the AI-generated selector is valid:

It replaces the primary selector
It is saved back to the selector store
self.store[name]["primary"] = new_selector

This allows the framework to “learn” over time.

**Example Usa...

unmatched.name: self-healing-crawler
unmatched.description: A production-quality web crawler that automatically detects when a website redesigns, invalidates stale CSS selectors, and uses Claude (LLM) to re-extract data and generate new selectors — all without any manual rule updates.
unmatched.readme:
Self-Healing Web Crawler

A production-quality web crawler that automatically detects when a website redesigns, invalidates stale CSS selectors, and uses Claude (LLM) to re-extract data and generate new selectors — all without any manual rule updates.

The Problem

CSS-selector-based scrapers break silently when websites redesign. Maintaining hundreds of scraper rules across dozens of domains is expensive, slow, and fragile. A redesign at 2am that flips all IDs to class names will go undetected until someone notices bad data hours later.

The Solution

A two-path extraction system that is both fast and resilient:

**Fast path (99% of requests):** Use cached CSS selectors — near-zero cost, ~1–5ms per page.
**Healing path (on drift or first visit):** Detect structural change via DOM fingerprinting, invalidate stale selectors, call the LLM to semantically re-extract and generate new selectors, cache them for the next visit.

---

System Architecture

 

---

Project Structure

 

---

Key Design Decisions

Structural Similarity: Jaccard on DOM Shingles

The original POC used XOR comparison on SHA256 hashes — fundamentally wrong. SHA256 is a cryptographic hash: a 1-character DOM change flips ~50% of output bits by design, making the similarity score meaningless (identical pages would score ~0.5).

**Fix:** DOM skeleton text is broken into overlapping 3-grams (shingles) of consecutive tag tokens. Jaccard similarity over these sets gives a true locality-sensitive score:

 

Identical DOM → 1.0
Text-only change (same tags, new content) → 1.0 (skeleton strips text)
Complete redesig...

🎛 maestro-skill - Generate Test Flows Easily

 

---

📋 About maestro-skill

maestro-skill is a tool that helps you create test flows for mobile and web apps. It works with many popular app types like React Native, Flutter, Swift, Kotlin, and Capacitor. You don’t need coding skills to use it. It lets you make tests that check if your app works well on phones or browsers.

This tool supports Android and iOS apps. It also works with web apps built on modern technology. You can use it to make tests that run on real devices or emulators. The main goal is to make testing easier and faster.

Before downloading, check your computer meets these needs:

At least 4 GB of RAM

If you want to test mobile apps, make sure your device is connected to your computer or you can run an emulator.

Run the Installer

Double-click the .exe file.
Follow the instructions in the setup wizard.
Accept the terms if asked.

unmatched.name: playwright-agent
unmatched.description: AI-powered Playwright test automation agent with self-healing selectors, registry-based element tracking, and LLM-driven code generation
unmatched.readme:
Playwright AI Test Agent

 

An AI-powered Playwright test automation framework with a **self-healing selector registry**, **CDP-based DOM discovery (Scout)**, and a **fully implemented orchestrator** that enforces a strict 4-layer architecture. The agent (Claude API) is a pure code translator — all deterministic logic lives in the orchestrator.

---

Architecture

 

| Layer | Path | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| Elements | src/elements/{Page}.elements.js | Locator builder functions only |
| Base Page | src/pages/BasePage.js | ActionEngine + AssertEngine (registry-aware) |
| Page Objects | src/pages/{Page}.page.js | All page-specific business methods |
| Tests | src/tests/{feature}/{name}.spec.js | describe/test + TestData.get() only |
| Config | config/ | Environment URLs + test data |

Orchestrator vs Agent (hard boundary)

| Concern | Owner |
|---|---|
| Input presence validation | Orchestrator (src/agent/orchestrator.js) |
| Stale index halt + ack | Orchestrator |
| Registry state resolution | Orchestrator |
| Scout element filtering | Orchestrator |
| Pending patch deduplication | Orchestrator |
| Tier 3 permission gate | Orchestrator |
| Post-call envelope validation | Orchestrator |
| File write + registry sync | Orchestrator |
| NL prompt → element matching | Agent |
| Locator tier selection | Agent |
| Code generation | Agent |
| Warning / clarification narration | Agent |

The orchestrator runs 7 deterministic gates before every Claude API call. The agent receives only clean, pre-resolved context and never re-validates inputs.

---

Self-Healing Registry

Ever...

unmatched.name: magpie-backend
unmatched.description: YAML-defined scrapers that self-heal via LLM + PR. One YAML = one spider (Scrapy or Playwright), content-addressed dedup, and a healer that re-derives broken selectors and opens a GitHub PR for review.
unmatched.readme:
🐦‍⬛ magpie-backend

> 🔧 **YAML-defined scrapers that self-heal via LLM + PR.**
> One config = one spider. When selectors break, an LLM patches them and opens a pull request.

🌐 Live API · 📖 Why · 🏗️ Architecture · 🎬 Demo

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

---

 

---

🎯 Why this exists

Scrapers break constantly. Selectors rot when sites redesign. The usual fix: someone notices days later, debugs manually, ships a patch. **magpie automates the detection-and-fix loop** while keeping humans in the review seat.

📝 **One YAML = one spider** — a factory pattern emits Scrapy (static) or Playwright (JS-rendered) from the same config schema; CSS **and** XPath selectors are both supported per-field.
🧬 **Self-healing via LLM + PR or in-place patch** — zero items (or a dead container selector) triggers a healer that re-fetches HTML, re-derives selectors via an LLM, validates them, and either opens a GitHub PR (for file-origin configs) or writes the fix directly to Postgres (for api-origin configs).
🔒 **No auto-merge for file-origin heals** — PRs labeled scrape:self-heal require human review.
🔑 **Content-addressed deduplication** — items are SHA-256 hashed with NFC normalization; runs produce diffs (new / updated / removed), not full dumps.
🛡️ **Strict config validation** — Pydantic v2 with extra="forbid" + compiled selector checks; typos and broken selectors fail at submit time, not at scrape time.
⚙️ **Async task queue** — scrapes run via Procrastinate (Postgres-backed) with retry + exponential backoff; a stale-run reaper cleans up after worker crashes.

---

✨ Features

🏭 YAML-dri...

unmatched.name: scrapling-rs
unmatched.description: Adaptive web scraping, built in Rust. A high-performance port of Python Scrapling.
unmatched.readme:
scrapling-rs

The Rust port of Scrapling, a web scraping framework that actually handles the messy reality of modern websites. Built for speed, built for stealth, built to keep working when sites change their HTML.

If you've used the Python version, you already know the API. If you haven't, here's the short version: Scrapling finds elements even after a website redesigns, impersonates real browsers so anti-bot systems can't tell you apart, and does it all fast enough to crawl thousands of pages concurrently.

This Rust port takes everything that makes Scrapling good and removes the performance ceiling. No GIL. No garbage collector. Native async. Single binary deployment.

What makes this different

Most scraping libraries break the moment a website changes a CSS class or moves a div. Scrapling doesn't. It saves a structural fingerprint of every element you care about and uses a 12-factor similarity algorithm to find it again, even when the surrounding HTML looks completely different. That's the adaptive engine, and it's the reason people use Scrapling over everything else.

The other big thing: real browser fingerprint impersonation. Not just setting a User-Agent header. Full TLS fingerprint emulation (JA3/JA4, HTTP/2 settings, cipher order) through 135+ browser profiles so anti-bot systems see Chrome, Firefox, or Safari instead of a Rust HTTP client.

Features

**HTML parsing and selection**
Fast DOM parsing via html5ever with CSS selector support, including ::text and ::attr() pseudo-elements
Full DOM navigation: parent, children, siblings, ancestors, descendants
Find el...

unmatched.name: product-scaper
unmatched.description: Product Scraper is a Python library that extracts structured product data from e-commerce websites using machine learning instead of fragile CSS selectors or XPaths. By combining visual rendering signals, text patterns, and DOM structure, it learns how products appear on a page and adapts to layout changes. It includes an interactive Playwright-bas
unmatched.readme:
Product Scraper 🛒

**Product Scraper** is a Python library for extracting structured product data from e-commerce websites. Instead of relying on fragile CSS selectors or hard-coded XPaths, it **learns how products appear on a page ** and mimics how a human identifies a title, price, or image.

By combining machine learning, visual cues, and DOM structure, Product Scraper adapts to different site layouts and remains resilient even when the HTML structure changes.

---

What Makes It Different?

Traditional scrapers break the moment a website tweaks its layout. **Product Scraper adapts.**

It analyzes:
**Visual Rendering:** Font size, font weight, and element position.
**Text Patterns:** Currencies, keywords (e.g., "add to cart"), and price formats.
**DOM Structure:** The spatial relationships between elements.

This multi-faceted approach allows the library to generalize across different pages and withstand minor layout updates.

---

🚀 Features

**Machine Learning Driven:** Uses a **Random Forest classifier** to identify product elements based on visual, textual, and structural features.
**Interactive Training:** Includes a **browser-based UI** (powered by Playwright). Simply open a page, click on the elements you want (titles, prices, images), and the system learns from your input—no complex XPath coding required.
**Automatic Product Grouping:** Detected elements are automatically grouped into coherent product objects using **spatial clustering**, ensuring the correct title is associated with the correct price and image.
**Resilient Scraping:** By learning patterns rath...

unmatched.name: gemini-bdd-dynamic-locators
unmatched.description: Revolutionizing Test Automation: Gemini LLM + BDD (Cucumber) Dynamic Locator Framework An AI-powered test automation framework that solves locator fragility by combining Google’s Gemini LLM with BDD (Cucumber). Ideal for React/Angular apps, it dynamically adapts XPath/CSS selectors, reducing maintenance and improving test stability.
unmatched.readme:
# 🚀 Gemini LLM with BDD (Cucumber) Based Dynamic Automation Solution

📜 Project Introduction

This project is an automation framework that uses a Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) approach to define test scenarios in plain language. It introduces an AI-powered solution to the "Locator Fragility" problem, which is one of the biggest and most common challenges in traditional automation solutions.

The project uses Google's Gemini language model to dynamically find web elements based on natural language descriptions, without relying on unstable attributes like ID, class, or fixed XPath. This makes our tests more resilient to UI changes while maintaining the human-readable and sustainable nature of BDD.

🔧 Tech Stack and Versions

**Java:** 21 (LTS)
**Build:** Maven
**Test Runner:** Cucumber + JUnit (Runner.java)
**Browser Driver:** ChromeDriver (recommended)

> Note: For Java 21 compilation, use maven-compiler-plugin (source/target 21).

 

---

📦 Installation

 

▶️ Running Tests

Run all scenarios:

 

Run by tag:

 

Environment and browser settings can be managed through config.properties.

---

🧭 Project Structure (Overview)

 

---

🤖 Core Idea: Dynamic, AI-Powered Locator

This framework stands out by **seamlessly integrating AI into a traditional test automation architecture**, combining the human-readable clarity of BDD with the adaptability of a large language model (LLM). Instead of relying on brittle, manually crafted locators, it leverages AI to dynamically generate stable selectors based on the context and intent of each step.

Natural language description...

mission: Externe Loesungsraum-Landkarte fuer "Der EventBaer-Worker braucht pro neuer Quelle manuelle Extraktor-Tuning-Arbeit: neue Selektoren hand-crafted, Edge-Cases patchen, neue Source-Families anlegen" ins Zielprojekt eventbear-worker ueberfuehren
Divergente Cluster (1) pruefen — hoeheres Lernpotenzial, hoeherer Transfer-Aufwand
deliverable: Je Cluster ein Kurz-Exposé mit Key-Takeaway fuer das Zielprojekt
Pro divergentem Cluster eine klare adopt/adapt/observe/ignore-Entscheidung
Decision-Log-Eintrag(e) im Zielprojekt, die auf diese Landscape referenzieren
context: Aktueller Ansatz: Paradigmen-Shift "Detect Once, Extract Many" in Umsetzung: Strategy-Registry + Pluggable Extractors. Aber die Onboarding-Kosten pro Quelle und das Fehlen eines echten Feedback/Lernsystems bleiben die dominanten Scaling-Blocker. Gesucht: externe Patterns fuer Wrapper-Maintenance, Template-Learning und adaptiven Selector-Recovery.

Erfolgskriterium: 100 neue Quellen in einem Batch laufen ohne per-Source Engineering durch,Selektor-Drift (Site-HTML-Änderung) wird automatisch erkannt und repariert statt stillschweigend leere Events zu liefern,Gelöste Extraktions-Regeln propagieren in einen Pattern-Bank, den ähnliche Quellen wiederverwenden,Neue Source-Families werden aus strukturellen Signalen vorgeschlagen statt hand-klassifiziert
Approach-Signature: self-healing, adaptive, wrapper-induction, pattern-bank, auto-onboarding, feedback-loop
Tech-Tags: nodejs, typescript, playwright, puppeteer, crawler, node
guardrails: Node.js-Worker mit gezielter Playwright-Eskalation, kein Python-Heavy-Stack
Open-Source-Referenzen bevorzugt — kein SaaS-Closed-Loop in der Kern-Pipeline
Lern-Entscheidungen müssen inspectable bleiben — kein Black-Box-ML im Hot-Path
uncertainties: Nicht-Ziel: Kein LLM-first-Extractor — heuristische + strukturelle Inferenz ist das Ziel
Nicht-Ziel: Keine generische Scraper-Library — die Frage ist System-Ebene, nicht Library-Wahl
Nicht-Ziel: Kein Orchestration-Tool (Airflow, Prefect) — die Lücke ist in Extraction/Normalization, nicht Scheduling

primary.implementationGoal: Adaptions-Skizze fuer Cluster "automation+data+query:adaptive-xpath" als Bruecke zum Zielprojekt
primary.firstSlice: Minimal-Integration: 1 Vertreter aus Cluster "automation+data+query:adaptive-xpath" gegen das Zielprojekt testen
compareChecklist: Wie unterscheidet sich dieser divergente Ansatz vom aktuellen Stand?
Welche Annahmen des aktuellen Ansatzes stellt dieses Cluster in Frage?
Ist der Transfer-Aufwand in Relation zum erwarteten Gewinn vertretbar?
stopIf: Performance oder Qualitaet unter dem aktuellen Niveau ohne klare Perspektive
Lizenz oder Distributionsform passt nicht zum Zielprojekt

compareChecklist: Wo unterscheidet sich dieser Pfad vom primaeren?
stopIf: Keine klaren Unterschiede zum primaeren Pfad
